name: Kubera Extended
description: Kubera, also known as Kuvera, Kuber, or Jambhala, is the Hindu god of wealth, treasure, and prosperity. He is considered the treasurer of the gods and the king of the Yakshas, benevolent nature spirits who guard treasures hidden in the earth and trees. Depicted as a stout figure adorned with jewels and holding a money pot or mongoose, Kubera's origins are traced back to the early Vedic period, though his prominence increased significantly during the Puranic era. He is often associated with Lakshmi, the goddess of wealth, and is considered her guardian and protector. Kubera's powers extend beyond mere material wealth; he is also believed to grant spiritual wealth and prosperity to his devotees. He resides in the city of Alaka in the Himalayas, a place of unimaginable riches and opulence. While generally benevolent, Kubera is also known for his immense pride and arrogance, often requiring humbling by other deities. He plays a significant role in various Hindu epics and scriptures, frequently interacting with gods and mortals alike. His worship is believed to bring financial stability, abundance, and overall well-being to those who seek his blessings. Kubera's influence extends beyond India, with variations of his worship found in Buddhism and Jainism, further solidifying his status as a powerful and universally revered deity of wealth and prosperity. He is also associated with the north direction, making him one of the Lokapalas, the guardians of the cardinal directions.
subtitle: God of Wealth and Prosperity

keyMyths.title: Kubera's Pride
keyMyths.description: Kubera, proud of his wealth, invited Shiva and Parvati for a feast. Shiva humbled him by having a small child, Ganesha, eat all the food, demonstrating the limitlessness of the divine.

keyMyths.title: Kubera and Vishnu
keyMyths.description: Once, Kubera borrowed a large sum from Vishnu and struggled to repay it. Vishnu, in the form of Venkateswara, is still repaying the debt, explaining the offerings received at the Tirupati temple.

keyMyths.title: Kubera's Penance
keyMyths.description: Kubera performed severe penance to please Shiva, who granted him the guardianship of all the wealth in the world, making him the god of riches and bestowing upon him the city of Alaka.
